scope.summary: Constitution rule 15 plus `swarm_discipline.sh verified-push`: the gate runs inside the push path, so a failing verdict never reaches `git push` and cannot be re-read as a pass.
out_of_scope: The four earlier incidents in the same session, carried by the sibling beads under oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.6.
The rust-oracledb half: its AGENTS.md still carries the twelve-rule constitution and none of these mechanisms.

The gate's own contents: --gate-cmd is required and unopinionated on purpose, because the script must not claim to know which gate you ran. AGENTS.md carries the documented workspace gate as the worked example.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh verified-push --gate-cmd ...
entry_points.trace: `--selftest` runs it with the incident's own failure text (`echo '!!! GATE FAILED' >&2; exit 1`) in a throwaway repo with no remote: it exits 65 before any push and writes {"status": "fail"} to the recorded verdict, which the selftest greps. A HEAD that moves while the gate runs is recorded as "stale" and refused for the same reason.

limitations: A gate that lies (exits 0 while printing a failure) is still believed: the mechanism binds the push to the exit status, which is the signal the 2026-07-21 incident ignored.
Not enforced by a git hook, so `git push` remains reachable directly; this is the honest path, not a locked door.
